meta.title: Kazemi - Your Personal Media Library
meta.description: Kazemi - A modern media player and library manager for iOS & macOS. Organize your video collection with a beautiful, intuitive interface.

hero.title: Kazemi
hero.subtitle: Your Personal Media Library
hero.kicker: Personal video library for iPhone, iPad and Mac
hero.description: A modern media player and library manager for iOS and macOS. Organize your personal video collection with a beautiful, intuitive interface.
hero.note: Built for your own media collection, local folders, and user-managed extensions.

desktop.heading: Library
desktop.emptyTitle: A place for your personal collection
desktop.emptyDescription: Home videos, trips, family events and saved downloads in one calm, native interface.

story.title: Built around your collection, not someone else’s platform
story.description: Kazemi is designed to feel like a real home for your media: clean browsing, polished playback, local folders, downloads, and optional user-managed extensions when you need more flexibility.

ownership.title: Own your library
ownership.description: Keep personal videos, archives, courses, films, or imported catalogs in one interface that feels native on Apple devices.

design.title: Designed like an app, not a file browser
design.description: Covers, metadata, history, subtitles, and downloads are presented in a clean interface instead of raw folders and filenames.

control.title: Flexible when you want it
control.description: Kazemi supports local media first, while still allowing user-imported JavaScript extensions for custom sources and extractors.

features.title: Features
features.subtitle: Everything you need for the perfect streaming experience

local.title: Local Media Library
local.description: Organize your video files in folders. Auto-detects items, clips, and subtitles.

extensions.title: JS Extension System
extensions.description: Customize your experience with JavaScript extensions. Secure, sandboxed, and easy to import.

browse.title: Browse & Filter
browse.description: Search and filter by genre, type, and more. Each source has its own catalog and filters.

library.title: Personal Library
library.description: Bookmark your favorite titles and track what you've watched. Your library syncs across devices.

downloads.title: Background Downloads
downloads.description: Save content for offline viewing. HLS and MP4 downloads with progress tracking.

subtitles.title: External Subtitles
subtitles.description: VTT subtitle support with language selection. Sync perfectly with your content.

theme.title: Dark/Light Theme
theme.description: Follows system appearance. Beautiful in any lighting condition.

languages.title: Multi-language UI
languages.description: Available in English, Spanish, and Chinese. More languages coming soon.

download.title: Download Kazemi
download.subtitle: Download it today from the App Store

appstore.title: Now on the App Store
appstore.description: Kazemi is now available for iPhone, iPad, and Mac on the App Store.

status.title: Official release
status.description: Live on the App Store. New updates and improvements are released there.

workflow.title: A simple flow that stays under your control
workflow.subtitle: Start with local media, expand with user-managed extensions only if you need them.

pick.title: Pick a folder
pick.description: Select a local folder on your device and let Kazemi organize videos, metadata, covers, and subtitles automatically.

import.title: Import what you need
import.description: Add JavaScript sources or extractors manually when you want additional catalogs or custom playback handling.

watch.title: Watch anywhere
watch.description: Play online or offline, keep history, manage downloads, and switch subtitle tracks without leaving the app.

docsTeaser.title: Need the extension syntax?
docsTeaser.description: The full JavaScript contract now lives in a dedicated Docs area, including source structure, extractor syntax, supported functions and implementation patterns.

sources.title: Source examples
sources.description: See the shape of `SOURCE`, list functions, details, children and mirrors.

extractors.title: Extractor examples
extractors.description: Understand how host resolvers declare domains and return streams.

patterns.title: Implementation patterns
patterns.description: Review practical patterns for stable parsing, errors, and resilient mirror handling.

docsPage.title: Kazemi Docs
docsPage.subtitle: JavaScript sources, extractors and extension syntax
docsPage.description: Everything you need to understand the current extension contract, build compatible sources, and apply practical implementation patterns.

overview.title: How Kazemi extensions are organized
overview.subtitle: Sources provide catalogs and metadata. Extractors resolve playable video URLs from hosts or embed pages.

sources.title: Source extensions
sources.description: A source exposes discovery functions like popular, latest and search, then returns detailed items, children and mirrors.

extractors.title: Extractor extensions
extractors.description: An extractor takes a host URL such as an embed page and resolves it into final playable streams like MP4 or HLS.

source.title: Basic source structure
source.subtitle: Every source starts with a `SOURCE` object plus a small set of async functions.

meta.title: 1. Define `SOURCE`
meta.description: Include stable metadata like id, name, baseUrl, language, version and optional iconUrl/contentKind.

browse.title: 2. Add browse functions
browse.description: Implement `fetchPopular`, `fetchLatest` and `fetchSearch` when available so the app can build lists and search results.

detail.title: 3. Return detail and children
detail.description: Use `fetchItemDetails(id)` and `fetchChildren(itemId)` to describe the media item and its playable parts.

functions.title: Current source contract
functions.subtitle: These are the main functions the runtime expects today.

cards.discovery: Return lightweight lists of media items for home sections and search results.
cards.detail: Return the full item plus its children, parts or entries using neutral naming.
cards.video: Return candidate mirrors or direct streams. Extractors may be used later to resolve those URLs.

extractors.title: Basic extractor structure
extractors.subtitle: Extractors are focused: detect supported domains and resolve a host URL into playable streams.

docsPage.footerTagline: Extension and integration documentation for Kazemi.

guides.title: Guides
guides.subtitle: Learn how to get the most out of Kazemi

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.description: Learn the basics of Kazemi and how to set up your first source.

importing.title: Importing Extensions
importing.description: Step-by-step guide to import and manage JavaScript extensions.

localLibrary.title: Local Library
localLibrary.description: Set up your local media folder and organize your offline content.

downloads.title: Downloads
downloads.description: Download content for offline viewing and manage your downloads.

subtitles.title: Subtitles
subtitles.description: Configure and manage external subtitle files for your content.

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.description: Common issues and solutions to help you get back on track.

install.title: Step 1: Install Kazemi
install.desc: Kazemi is now available on the App Store. Visit our Download section for the direct link.

open.title: Step 2: Open the App
open.desc: Launch Kazemi from your home screen. On first launch, the app will request notification permissions (optional).

explore.title: Step 3: Explore the Interface
tabs: Library - Your saved titles and bookmarks
Updates - New additions from your library

extensions.title: Step 4: Add Extensions
extensions.desc: Go to Settings and import JavaScript extensions to add new content sources. See our Importing Extensions guide for details.

importingExtensions.title: Importing Extensions
importingExtensions.intro: Kazemi uses a JavaScript extension system. Extensions are plain .js files that add new functionality to the app.
types: Source Extensions - Browse catalogs from different websites
Extractor Extensions - Resolve video URLs from embed pages

find.title: Step 1: Find an Extension
find.desc: Extensions are .js files that can be shared via direct download links or local files.

settings.title: Step 2: Open Settings
settings.desc: In Kazemi, navigate to the Settings tab and find the Extensions section.

import.title: Step 3: Import
options: Import from URL - Paste a direct link to the .js file
Import from File - Select a .js file from your device

verify.title: Step 4: Verify
verify.desc: The extension will be validated and added to your installed extensions list. It's now ready to use!

importingExtensions.managingDesc: You can disable or remove extensions at any time from the Settings menu. Removed extensions are permanently deleted from the app.

localLibrary.enableDesc: Go to Settings → Local Library and toggle it ON.
localLibrary.selectDesc: Tap "Select Folder" and navigate to your media folder. Grant permission when prompted.
localLibrary.browseDesc: Your local content will appear in the Sources tab under "Local". Tap to browse and play!
localLibrary.infoJsonDesc: Add an info.json file in each media folder for metadata like title, synopsis, tags, and year.

downloads.title: Downloads
downloads.intro: Download content for offline viewing with Kazemi's built-in download manager.

find.title: Step 1: Find Content
find.desc: Browse to the item you want to download.

tap.title: Step 2: Tap Download
tap.desc: On the item detail page, tap the download button (↓) in the toolbar.

quality.title: Step 3: Choose Quality
quality.desc: Select your preferred video quality and mirror. Downloads with an extractor icon are recommended.

monitor.title: Step 4: Monitor Progress
monitor.desc: Check download progress in the Downloads view (Settings → Downloads). Downloads continue in the background!

managingItems: Play - Tap a completed download to play offline
Delete - Swipe left or long-press to remove downloads
Batch Download - Select multiple items and download them at once

subtitles.title: Subtitles
subtitles.intro: Kazemi supports external VTT subtitle files for your content.
subtitles.onlineDesc: Many sources provide subtitles automatically. When available, you'll see a CC button in the player to toggle subtitles on/off.

name.title: Step 1: Name Your Files
name.desc: Name subtitle files the same as your video files, with a .vtt extension:

place.title: Step 2: Place in Same Folder
place.desc: Keep the subtitle file in the same folder as the video.

detect.title: Step 3: Automatic Detection
detect.desc: Kazemi will automatically detect and load matching subtitle files when you play the video.

subtitles.controlsDesc: While playing, tap the CC button to:

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.intro: Having issues? Here are some common solutions.

extensionsNotLoading.title: Extensions Not Loading
items: Ensure the extension follows the Kazemi extension format

videoWontPlay.title: Video Won't Play
items: The extractor for that server may be unavailable

downloadsFailing.title: Downloads Failing
items: Ensure the app has background app refresh enabled

localLibraryNotScanning.title: Local Library Not Scanning
items: Check that files are in supported formats (.mp4, .m4v, .mkv, .mov)

troubleshooting.stillHavingIssuesDesc: Visit our GitHub Issues page to report bugs or request help.
